title: Rolling Stone's 2026 Grammy Song of the Year Prediction
text:
Rolling Stone's 2026 Grammy Song of the Year Prediction

Predicted Winner: Lady Gaga – "Abracadabra"

Why They're Picking Her: Rolling Stone describes the track as "the Gaga-est song ever recorded," noting it features self-referential elements and gothic imagery that make it a standout. Despite four previous nominations, Gaga has never won in this category, making a victory here particularly significant.

Key Contenders:
- Sabrina Carpenter – "Manchild" (Rolling Stone's "Who Should Win" pick) - Debuted at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100
- Kendrick Lamar ft. SZA – "Luther" - Described as having "momentum for days"
- Bad Bunny – "DtMF" - Could be a spoiler, especially given his Super Bowl LX performance slot
- Rosé & Bruno Mars – "APT."
- Billie Eilish – "Wildflower"
- Doechii – "Anxiety"
- Huntr/x – "Golden" (from Netflix's KPop Demon Hunters)

Industry expert Alex Tear calls this year's nominees "world-class," highlighting the exceptional talent across all categories.
